title: Length-resolved Operator Growth and Path-Entropy Obstructions to Many-Body Localization
abstract: For the disordered Ising chain with transverse and longitudinal fields, where couplings and fields are drawn from strictly positive distributions, Cao~\cite{Cao} has shown that the moments $μ_{2k} = \|[H,σ^z_0]^{(k)}\|_2^2$ grow almost factorially, $μ_{2k}^{1/(2k)}\sim k/\ln k$, and thus asymptotically at the maximal allowed rate. We generalize this result by resolving the operator norm in support length and show that the weight at length $\ell_k \sim k/\ln k$ already exhibits almost factorial growth, $\|[H,σ^z_0]^{(k)}_{\ell_k}\|_2 \gtrsim (k/\ln k)^k$. This implies maximal spatial delocalization of local operators and, in particular, rules out dynamical locality -- the strongest form of many-body localization -- at any disorder strength. We further establish rigorously a finite-size crossover scale $L\sim (W/J)^2$, where $W$ is the disorder and $J$ the coupling strength. For $L\lesssim (W/J)^2$ numerical studies only access a pre-asymptotic regime. Finally, we identify a structural path-entropy obstruction to perturbative LIOM constructions, based on the almost factorial branching of operator content and independent of resonance effects; the same mechanism strongly suggests ballistic real-time operator spreading, so sub-ballistic or localized dynamics would require a presently unidentified cancellation principle acting on almost factorially many disorder-dependent paths with random amplitudes.
summary: This paper establishes a structural obstruction to Many-Body Localization in disordered quantum spin chains by analyzing the almost factorial growth of local operators. By resolving this growth across spatial support lengths, it proves that dynamical locality is impossible for generic disorder strengths. The findings suggest that such systems are asymptotically ergodic unless an unknown cancellation principle stabilizes them.
main_problem: The paper investigates Many-Body Localization (MBL) by analyzing the growth rate of local operators via iterated commutators in disordered quantum spin chains.
main_result: It rigorously proves that operator norms exhibit almost factorial growth, which rules out dynamical locality and suggests that generic disordered systems are asymptotically ergodic.
method: The analysis uses commutator expansion techniques, resolving operator norms based on support length ($\ell$), and introducing path-entropy arguments to constrain the possible dynamics.
model_or_system: The primary model is a disordered Ising chain with transverse and longitudinal fields. The analysis is generalized to abstract LIOM Hamiltonians using Pauli string expansions.
key_observables: Operator norms ($\| \cdot \|$), moments $\mu_{2k}$, length-resolved operator weights, and path entropy.
important_parameters: Disorder strength ($W$), coupling strength ($J$), and the finite-size crossover scale $L \sim (W/J)^2$.
main_assumptions: The analysis often assumes strictly positive couplings to derive rigorous lower bounds; it also relies on identifying a structural path-entropy obstruction.
figures_summary: Not specified, but the notes indicate comparisons between theoretical growth rates and finite-size numerical access limits.
paper_structure: The paper progresses from establishing almost factorial growth in moments (Cao's result generalization) to resolving this growth locally ($\ell$), which leads to ruling out dynamical locality. It then identifies a structural path-entropy obstruction against perturbative constructions of Local Integrals of Motion (LIOMs).
why_interesting: The work provides deep theoretical constraints on the stability of MBL, suggesting that generic disorder drives systems towards ergodic behavior by overwhelming any potential localization mechanism through operator growth.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
